title: Automate weekly RevOps board prep
problem: Every Sunday a RevOps analyst spends 4 hours assembling the Monday board read: pipeline coverage, top movers, slipped deals, and segment-level commit. Most of it could be auto-generated.
currentProcess: Manual Salesforce exports + a Tableau extract → pasted into a Google Slides template → Slack-shared by 8am Monday.
desiredOutcome: Auto-generated Monday-morning board read posted to #revops by 7am, with the analyst reviewing rather than authoring.

title: Customer onboarding milestone tracker
problem: Our PS team manages 20+ concurrent onboardings and has no single view of milestone status. Every update is a Slack ping to the CSM. Customers are asking for progress visibility we can't give.
currentProcess: Manual status updates in a shared Notion doc, updated ad hoc.
burdenEstimate: ~45 min/day per PM in status coordination
desiredOutcome: A structured milestone tracker with customer-facing view and automated status updates when key events complete.

title: Pipeline coverage early-warning email
problem: Marketing and GTM leaders only learn about pipeline coverage gaps at the weekly forecast review — by then the window to act has closed. We want a daily digest that flags any segment slipping below 3x coverage with the responsible AE and the at-risk amount.
currentProcess: Coverage is reviewed manually in Tableau every Wednesday during the forecast call. No proactive alerting.
desiredOutcome: Daily 7am email to regional VPs with coverage by segment, gap-to-target, and top deals contributing to the gap.
decisionResponse: Thanks for submitting "Pipeline coverage early-warning email". We're accepting this. A SWAT engineer will reach out within the week to discuss requirements.

title: Renewal risk dashboard
problem: The renewal team has no proactive view of accounts at risk before the 90-day window. By the time they react, the customer has already made up their mind.
currentProcess: Manual review of CSM health scores + ad hoc Salesforce queries. No systematic early warning.
desiredOutcome: A dashboard surfacing at-risk renewals 120+ days out, with signals on usage, support, and engagement.
decisionResponse: Thanks for submitting "Renewal risk dashboard". We're accepting this. A SWAT engineer will reach out within the week to discuss requirements.

title: AI sales call summarizer
problem: Reps spend 20–30 minutes after every call writing notes in Salesforce. Most of it is reconstructing what was said. An AI summary tied to the recording would save the bulk of that time.
currentProcess: Manual note-taking + Gong recording. Notes are inconsistent quality across reps.
desiredOutcome: Auto-generated call summary attached to the Salesforce opportunity with key topics, objections, and next steps.
decisionResponse: Thanks for submitting "AI sales call summarizer". We're not picking this up right now due to capacity constraints. Expected revisit: Q3 2026. In the meantime, you might try reviewing existing solutions in the catalog for forkable patterns. You can re-submit if priority or resourcing changes.

title: Internal Wikipedia builder
problem: Our internal docs are scattered across Notion, Confluence, and Slack threads. We need an internal-search-and-aggregation tool, basically a Wikipedia-but-for-C3.
currentProcess: Search Notion + Confluence separately. Slack threads are not indexed.
burdenEstimate: Hard to estimate; everyone loses ~10 min/day looking for things
desiredOutcome: A unified search + aggregation experience over Notion, Confluence, and Slack.
decisionResponse: Thanks for submitting "Internal Wikipedia builder". After review, this isn't something SWAT will take on because the scope exceeds our team's mandate for internal tooling. We'd suggest raising this through the standard engineering planning process.
